title: 1.1: Adds an optional parameter to SyncEvent.mark_as_taken for claiming stale events

annotations.description: Verifies that SyncEvent.mark_as_taken in app/models.py accepts an optional parameter allow_taken_older_than of type Optional[Arrow] (defaulting to None) and returns bool, matching the interface specification.
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` line 16: `app/models.py` signature changes from mark_as_taken(self) to mark_as_taken(self, allow_taken_older_than: Optional[Arrow] = None) -> bool.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states mark_as_taken should 'handle not just unclaimed events but also events that have been sitting in a taken state for too long, while still preserving its current behavior for existing callers that do not need that capability', which requires a new optional parameter. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface section of the prompt specifies the exact contract: Input 'self, allow_taken_older_than: Optional[Arrow] = None' and Output 'bool'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must add this optional parameter to mark_as_taken matching the interface contract.

title: 1.2: Enables claiming events whose taken_time is older than a given threshold

annotations.description: Verifies that when allow_taken_older_than is provided, mark_as_taken can successfully claim events that have a non-NULL taken_time older than the threshold, in addition to still claiming unclaimed (NULL taken_time) events.
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` lines 17-24: `app/models.py` when allow_taken_older_than is provided, the WHERE clause includes (taken_time IS NULL OR taken_time < :taken_older_than).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'SyncEvent.mark_as_taken() only works for events with a NULL taken_time, which means events that were previously claimed by a stalled or crashed runner get stuck forever and are never retried'. [3: Logical Necessity] Dead letter events returned by get_dead_letter() have non-NULL taken_time; if mark_as_taken cannot claim them, the dead letter recovery path is broken. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must allow claiming events with stale taken_time when a threshold is provided.

title: 1.3: Preserves default mark_as_taken behavior for callers that do not pass a threshold

annotations.description: Verifies that when allow_taken_older_than is not provided (defaults to None), mark_as_taken behaves identically to the original implementation: it only claims events where taken_time IS NULL. Existing callers such as PostgresEventSource (events/event_source.py line 62) must continue to work without modification.
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` line 17: `app/models.py` the base condition 'taken_time IS NULL' is always present; the stale threshold condition is only added when allow_taken_older_than is provided.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states mark_as_taken should handle stale events 'while still preserving its current behavior for existing callers that do not need that capability'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] PostgresEventSource at events/event_source.py line 62 calls mark_as_taken() without arguments and relies on it rejecting already-taken events to prevent duplicate processing. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must preserve the default NULL-only claiming behavior when no threshold is passed.

title: 1.4: Extracts dead letter event processing logic into a separate DeadLetterEventSource.execute_loop method

annotations.description: Verifies that the event fetching, claiming, and dispatching logic is extracted from the infinite loop in DeadLetterEventSource.run into a new method named execute_loop, matching the interface specification. The method should accept on_event: Callable[[SyncEvent], NoReturn] and return list[SyncEvent].
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` lines 36-51: `events/event_source.py` a new execute_loop method is defined with signature (self, on_event: Callable[[SyncEvent], NoReturn]) -> list[SyncEvent].
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the event processing logic should also be separated from the infinite loop so it can be called and tested on its own'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface section of the prompt specifies DeadLetterEventSource.execute_loop with Input: 'self, on_event: Callable[[SyncEvent], NoReturn]' and Output: 'list[SyncEvent]'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must extract the processing logic into execute_loop matching the interface contract.

title: 1.5: Claims each dead letter event via mark_as_taken before dispatching it in execute_loop

annotations.description: Verifies that inside execute_loop, each dead letter event is claimed by calling mark_as_taken before being dispatched to the callback, and that only successfully claimed events are dispatched.
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` lines 48-50: `events/event_source.py` for each event, mark_as_taken(allow_taken_older_than=threshold) is called, and on_event is only invoked if it returns True.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the dead letter source should claim each event before processing it, and only process events it successfully claimed'. [3: Logical Necessity] Without claiming before dispatching, multiple runners can still process the same event concurrently, which is the race condition the prompt asks to fix. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must claim each event and check the result before dispatching.

annotations.description: Verifies that the run method's infinite loop delegates event processing to execute_loop rather than containing the fetch/claim/dispatch logic inline. The run method should call self.execute_loop(on_event) and use its return value to determine whether to sleep.
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` line 71: `events/event_source.py` the run method's try block is reduced to 'events = self.execute_loop(on_event)' followed by Session.close() and the sleep logic.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires separating processing logic from the infinite loop. [3: Logical Necessity] If run does not delegate to execute_loop, the extraction has no effect and the method cannot be called or tested independently. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must have run call execute_loop to perform the actual event processing.

title: 1.7: Creates the oneshot backfill script at oneshot/send_plan_change_events.py

annotations.description: Verifies that a new Python script exists at oneshot/send_plan_change_events.py that can be executed as a standalone script.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'we also need a oneshot backfill script at oneshot/send_plan_change_events.py'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must create this file at the specified path.

title: 1.8: Iterates over PartnerUser records in batches in the oneshot script

annotations.description: Verifies that the oneshot script queries PartnerUser records from the database and processes them in batches rather than loading all records at once.
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` lines 113-118: `oneshot/send_plan_change_events.py` PartnerUser records are queried in batches using a range-based filter.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the script 'should go through PartnerUser records in batches'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] PartnerUser (app/models.py line 3652) is a database model; loading all records at once would be impractical for large datasets. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must process PartnerUser records in batches.

title: 1.9: Checks each user's active subscription end time excluding partner subscriptions

annotations.description: Verifies that for each PartnerUser, the script retrieves the user's active subscription end time with partner subscriptions excluded (e.g. by calling get_active_subscription_end with include_partner_subscription=False or equivalent logic).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the script should 'check each user's active subscription end time (excluding partner subscriptions)'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The User model (app/models.py line 729) has get_active_subscription_end(include_partner_subscription: bool) which is the existing API for this. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must retrieve subscription end time while excluding partner subscriptions.

title: 1.10: Dispatches a UserPlanChanged event with plan_end_time for each PartnerUser

annotations.description: Verifies that the script constructs a UserPlanChanged event with the appropriate plan end time and dispatches it (e.g. via EventDispatcher.send_event or equivalent) for each PartnerUser record processed.
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` lines 127-130: `oneshot/send_plan_change_events.py` creates UserPlanChanged(plan_end_time=end_timestamp) and dispatches via EventDispatcher.send_event.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the script should 'fire a UserPlanChanged event with the appropriate plan end time'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] UserPlanChanged is defined in app/events/generated/event_pb2.py and EventDispatcher.send_event (app/events/event_dispatcher.py line 49) is the existing dispatch mechanism. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must dispatch UserPlanChanged events with plan_end_time for each PartnerUser.

title: 1.11: Supports command line arguments for PartnerUser ID range in the oneshot script

annotations.description: Verifies that the oneshot script accepts command line arguments to specify a start and end PartnerUser ID range (e.g. via argparse, sys.argv, or similar CLI parsing).
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` lines 92-100: `oneshot/send_plan_change_events.py` uses argparse with --start_pu_id and --end_pu_id arguments.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the script 'should support command line arguments for the PartnerUser ID range'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must accept CLI arguments for specifying the ID range to process.

title: 1.12: Prints progress as the oneshot script runs

annotations.description: Verifies that the oneshot script outputs progress information to stdout as it processes batches, so the operator can monitor execution.
annotations.evidence: `gold.patch` lines 108, 140-142: `oneshot/send_plan_change_events.py` prints starting range and per-batch progress.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the script should 'print progress as it runs'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must print progress output during batch processing.

title: 4.1: Removes or breaks the existing run method's loop, sleep, or error handling behavior in DeadLetterEventSource

annotations.description: Detects if the solution removes, replaces, or breaks the existing infinite loop structure in DeadLetterEventSource.run, including its sleep-when-idle behavior, exception handling, or Session.close() call. The refactor should extract processing logic into execute_loop but must preserve run's role as the long-lived loop with error recovery.
annotations.evidence: Codebase `events/event_source.py` lines 88-112: run contains a while True loop with try/except, Session.close(), conditional sleep, and error logging that must be preserved.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks to 'separate the event processing logic from the infinite loop', implying the loop itself must remain intact. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] DeadLetterEventSource.run is called by the event runner infrastructure and must remain a long-lived blocking loop with error recovery, as it is the entry point for dead letter processing. [5: Conclusion] Removing or breaking run's loop structure, sleep logic, or error handling breaks the dead letter processing lifecycle.
